message:
feat(algorithms,python): add 10 new graph algorithms and fix directed shortest_path routing

Add native Rust implementations for the following algorithms in
fnx-algorithms, with corresponding PyO3 Python bindings in fnx-python:

- dfs_labeled_edges: DFS edge classification (tree/back/forward/cross)
  for undirected graphs from a given source node.
- generalized_degree: Per-node distribution mapping triangle counts to
  the number of neighbors sharing that many triangles.
- is_strongly_regular: Checks the strongly regular property (n,k,λ,μ)
  by verifying uniform degree and consistent common-neighbor counts for
  adjacent and non-adjacent pairs.
- flow_hierarchy_directed: Fraction of edges not participating in any
  cycle, computed via Tarjan's SCC decomposition on DiGraph.
- power: k-th power graph construction using BFS from every node up to
  distance k, returning a new Graph with the same node set.
- square_clustering_map: Square (4-cycle) clustering coefficient per
  node, measuring the fraction of possible squares through each vertex.
- ego_graph / ego_graph_directed: BFS-based ego subgraph extraction
  within a given radius for both Graph and DiGraph.
- common_neighbor_centrality: CCPA link-prediction index combining
  common-neighbor count with shortest-path-weighted bonus.
- degree_mixing_dict / degree_mixing_dict_directed: Edge counts keyed
  by (source_degree, target_degree) pairs.
- connected_dominating_set: Greedy connected dominating set starting
  from the highest-degree node.

Also adds fnx-runtime as a dependency in fnx-algorithms/Cargo.toml.

Fix shortest_path for directed graphs (DiGraph/MultiDiGraph):
Previously, the unweighted branch always extracted the undirected view,
which meant directed shortest paths were computed over the symmetrized
edge set—producing incorrect results and missing valid directed-only
paths. The fix checks for a digraph first and delegates to new
`compute_single_shortest_path_directed` /
`compute_single_source_shortest_paths_directed` helpers that respect
edge direction. The (None, Some(target)) case for digraphs now
correctly computes paths from every node to the target instead of
reversing paths from the target (which is only valid for undirected
graphs).

Python bindings added for: generalized_degree_rust,
is_strongly_regular_rust, flow_hierarchy_rust, power_rust,
square_clustering_rust — all registered in the algorithms module and
using py.allow_threads for GIL release.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
